话.origin: The speech radical 讠 next to a tongue 舌 — words made by a wagging tongue become talk.

放.origin: On the left 方 gives the sound, and on the right 攵 is a hand that lets something go free.

sentence.en: We finish school at three o'clock.

完.origin: A roof 宀 over 元 (the first one) — when everything is tucked safely inside, the job is finished.

觉.origin: Little marks on top sit over an eye 见 that sees — when you see clearly, you feel and become aware.

滴.origin: The water radical 氵 means this is about water — a tiny drop of water falling down.

sentence.en: Water drips down from the roof.

答.origin: Bamboo ⺮ over 合 (to join) — long ago answers were written on bamboo slips that fit together.

芽.origin: Grass 艹 on top shows it is a plant, and 牙 (tooth) gives the sound — a tiny tooth-like sprout.

苗.origin: Grass 艹 growing up out of a field 田 — a young green seedling.

sentence.en: There are many rice seedlings in the field.

种.origin: The grain radical 禾 with 中 (middle) for the sound — seeds you plant in the ground.

青.origin: Think of fresh young plants under the sky — 青 is the blue-green color of nature.

sentence.en: The mountain is covered with green grass.

沙.origin: Water 氵 beside 少 (few, tiny) — sand is countless little grains left by the water.

当.origin: Tiny strokes ⺌ over 彐 — picture standing right in the proper place at the right time.

sentence.en: When I grow up I want to be a teacher.

号.origin: A mouth 口 above 丂 calling out — a loud cry, and later a number or sign you announce.

能.origin: A bear shape — head 厶, body ⺼, and strong claws 匕 — a mighty bear that is able to do anything.

句.origin: A wrapping shape 勹 curling around a mouth 口 — words from the mouth wrapped into one sentence.

串.origin: Two mouths 口 poked through by a line 丨 — like beads threaded together on a string.

sentence.en: There is a bunch of grapes on the table.

以.origin: A person 人 stands beside a tool — 以 means using something to get a task done.

省.origin: Fewer 少 over an eye 目 — looking closely with small eyes means to save and to examine.

成.origin: A guard with a spear 戈 watching over the work — when the job is well done, it is accomplished.

江.origin: Water 氵 beside 工 (work) for the sound — a very big, wide river.

sentence.en: There are many fish in the river.

河.origin: The water radical 氵 with 可 giving the sound — a flowing river or stream.

sentence.en: The water in the little river is clear.

古.origin: Words passed down through ten 十 mouths 口 — stories so old they come from long ago.

国.origin: Precious jade 玉 kept safe inside the borders 囗 — a treasured country.

条.origin: Picture a long thin branch of wood 木 below — 条 counts long, narrow things like a strip or a fish.

它.origin: A roof 宀 over 匕 — long ago this was a snake under a roof; now 它 simply means 'it'.

sentence.en: This is my cat; it is very cute.

名.origin: At night 夕 you call out 口 to say who you are — that is your name.

最.origin: Under the sun 日, taking 取 the top spot — 最 means the most of all.

亲.origin: Think of family standing close 立 and small 小 together — the people dearest and nearest to you.

sentence.en: I am very close to my mother.

从.origin: One person 人 walking right behind another 人 — following along, going from here to there.

华.origin: Picture a plant bursting into bloom — 华 means flowery, splendid, and also stands for China.

sentence.en: I am a child of China.

等.origin: Bamboo strips ⺮ set out before a court 寺 in order — sorted by rank, and you wait your turn.

sentence.en: Please wait for me a moment.

各.origin: Feet walking 夂 above a mouth 口 — everyone arrives their own way, so 各 means each one.

sentence.en: There are all kinds of flowers in the park.

香.origin: Grain 禾 over the sun 日 — ripe grain warmed by the sun gives off a sweet, lovely smell.

茶.origin: The grass radical 艹 shows it is a plant — tea leaves you pick and brew into a warm drink.

汤.origin: The water radical 氵 tells you it is liquid — hot, tasty soup in a bowl.

sentence.en: This bowl of soup is delicious.

虾.origin: The insect radical 虫 (used for small creatures) with 下 for the sound — a little shrimp.

饱.origin: The food radical 饣 with 包 (wrap) for the sound — your tummy wrapped full and satisfied.

顿.origin: The head radical 页 with 屯 for the sound — you nod your head and pause, and it also counts meals.

才.origin: Picture a little sprout pushing up through the ground — a budding talent just beginning to grow.

意.origin: A sound 音 over a heart 心 — what your heart says inside is your idea or meaning.
